data.title: Discovering Majuli
data.description: Majuli is the world's largest river island known for its eco-friendly lifestyle and rich Assamese culture.

data.title: Discovering Kaziranga National Park
data.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site famous for sheltering two-thirds of the world's great one-horned rhinoceroses.

data.title: Discovering Manas National Park
data.description: A biosphere reserve and UNESCO Heritage site offering dense jungles and sightings of tigers and elephants.

data.title: Discovering Kamakhya Temple
data.description: One of the oldest of the 51 Shakti Pithas, located on the Nilachal Hill in Guwahati.

data.title: Discovering Haflong
data.description: Assam's only hill station, characterized by rolling mountains, lush green valleys, and pristine lakes.

data.title: Discovering Dibru-Saikhowa National Park
data.description: A national park renowned for its feral horses and vast wetlands.

data.title: Discovering Nameri National Park
data.description: Nestled in the foothills of the Eastern Himalayas, known for its elephants and thrilling river activities.

data.title: Discovering Pobitora Wildlife Sanctuary
data.description: Known for having the highest density of one-horned rhinoceroses in the world.

data.title: Discovering Sivasagar
data.description: The former capital of the Ahom Kingdom, featuring magnificent ancient palaces and temples.

data.title: Discovering Orang National Park
data.description: Often called mini Kaziranga, known for rhinos, tigers, and a rich variety of fish.

data.title: Discovering Tawang
data.description: Famous for the largest Buddhist monastery in India and breathtaking high-altitude landscapes.

data.title: Discovering Ziro Valley
data.description: A picturesque valley known for its lush pine hills, terraced rice fields, and the indigenous Apatani tribe.

data.title: Discovering Namdapha National Park
data.description: A biodiversity hotspot and tiger reserve with diverse ecosystems ranging from tropical to alpine.

data.title: Discovering Bomdila
data.description: Offers brilliant landscape views of the snow-clad Himalayas alongside sprawling apple orchards.

data.title: Discovering Sela Pass
data.description: A high-altitude mountain pass providing spectacular views and serving as the gateway to Tawang.

data.title: Discovering Dirang
data.description: A charming valley town famous for its hot water springs and ancient dzongs.

data.title: Discovering Mechuka
data.description: An offbeat destination known for its pristine beauty, snow-capped peaks, and the Siyom River.

data.title: Discovering Roing
data.description: Known for the serene Mehao Lake and the ancient Bhismaknagar Fort.

data.title: Discovering Pasighat
data.description: The oldest town of Arunachal Pradesh, situated on the banks of the mighty Siang River.

data.title: Discovering Bhalukpong
data.description: A beautiful town bordering Assam, known for the Kameng River and orchid reserves.

data.title: Discovering Dzukou Valley
data.description: Famous for its rolling hills, seasonal wildflowers, and incredibly scenic trekking trails.

data.title: Discovering Kisama Heritage Village
data.description: The venue for the famous Hornbill Festival, designed to protect and promote traditional Naga culture.

data.title: Discovering Kohima War Cemetery
data.description: A memorial dedicated to soldiers of the Allied Forces who died in the Second World War at Kohima.

data.title: Discovering Khonoma Village
data.description: India's first green village, known for its terraced agriculture and deep-rooted conservation efforts.

data.title: Discovering Japfu Peak
data.description: The second-highest peak in Nagaland offering panoramic views of the Dzukou Valley.

data.title: Discovering Mokokchung
data.description: The cultural and intellectual center of the Ao Naga tribe, offering scenic views and rich traditions.

data.title: Discovering Tuophema Village
data.description: A tourist village offering an authentic Naga lifestyle experience in traditional huts.

data.title: Discovering Mon
data.description: The land of the Konyak Nagas, famous for their tattooed faces and traditional wood carving.

data.title: Discovering Wokha
data.description: The land of the Lotha tribe and famous for the Amur Falcon roosting site near Doyang Reservoir.

data.title: Discovering Pfutsero
data.description: The highest altitude town in Nagaland, known for its cold weather and stunning cherry blossoms.

data.title: Discovering Cherrapunji (Sohra)
data.description: Historic wettest place on earth, famous for dramatic waterfalls, cliffs, and living root bridges.

data.title: Discovering Dawki
data.description: A border town acclaimed for the crystal-clear Umngot River where boats seem to float on air.

data.title: Discovering Mawlynnong
data.description: Dubbed Asia's cleanest village, it is a model for eco-friendly living and community-driven sanitation.

data.title: Discovering Nongriat Double Decker Root Bridge
data.description: A spectacular natural wonder requiring a challenging hike to view bridges formed entirely of tree roots.

data.title: Discovering Laitlum Canyon
data.description: Translating to 'End of the Hills', this canyon offers dramatic, sweeping views of misty valleys.

data.title: Discovering Umiam Lake
data.description: A sprawling man-made reservoir surrounded by sylvan hills, acting as a gateway to Shillong.

data.title: Discovering Mawsynram
data.description: Currently holding the record for the highest average rainfall globally, featuring lush green landscapes.

data.title: Discovering Krag Suri Falls
data.description: One of the most aesthetically pleasing waterfalls in Meghalaya, known for its deep blue pool.

data.title: Discovering Balpakram National Park
data.description: A pristine national park in the Garo Hills, known for its deep gorge and rich biodiversity.

data.title: Discovering Nokrek National Park
data.description: A UNESCO biosphere reserve protecting the red panda and wild Asian elephants.

data.title: Discovering Gangtok
data.description: The capital of Sikkim, offering stunning views of Mt. Kanchenjunga and vibrant Tibetan culture.

data.title: Discovering Tsomgo Lake
data.description: A spectacular glacial lake situated at a high altitude, changing colors with the seasons.

data.title: Discovering Nathula Pass
data.description: A mountain pass on the Indo-China border that was once a part of the ancient Silk Route.

data.title: Discovering Pelling
data.description: A serene town providing the closest and most breathtaking views of the Kanchenjunga range.

data.title: Discovering Lachung
data.description: A picturesque mountain village in North Sikkim serving as the base for Yumthang Valley.

data.title: Discovering Lachen
data.description: A base camp for Gurudongmar Lake, offering rugged terrain and pristine alpine views.

data.title: Discovering Yumthang Valley
data.description: Also known as the Valley of Flowers of the East, surrounded by snow-capped mountains.

data.title: Discovering Gurudongmar Lake
data.description: One of the highest lakes in the world, considered sacred by Buddhists, Sikhs, and Hindus.

data.title: Discovering Ravangla
data.description: Known for the magnificent Buddha Park and undisturbed views of the Himalayas.

data.title: Discovering Namchi
data.description: The cultural capital of Sikkim featuring replicas of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites.

data.title: Discovering Loktak Lake
data.description: The largest freshwater lake in Northeast India, famous for its circular floating phumdis.

data.title: Discovering Kangla Fort
data.description: The old palace of the Kingdom of Manipur, rich in history, archaeology, and local mythology.

data.title: Discovering Shirui Hills
data.description: Famous for the rare Shirui Lily flower which blooms only on the peak of this hill.

data.title: Discovering Andro Village
data.description: An ancient village known for its unique traditional pottery and the cultural heritage complex.

data.title: Discovering Khongjom War Memorial
data.description: A tribute to the martyrs of the historic Anglo-Manipuri War of 1891.

data.title: Discovering Reiek Tlang
data.description: A picturesque hill offering panoramic views of Aizawl and a model Mizo heritage village.

data.title: Discovering Champhai
data.description: A vibrant commercial town known as the Rice Bowl of Mizoram, offering stunning hilly terrain.

data.title: Discovering Vantawng Falls
data.description: The highest uninterrupted waterfall in Mizoram, surrounded by thick, lush bamboo forests.

data.title: Discovering Unakoti
data.description: An ancient pilgrimage center with massive rock-cut sculptures and stone images of Hindu deities.

data.title: Discovering Neermahal
data.description: A stunning water palace built in the middle of Rudrasagar Lake, showcasing Hindu and Islamic architecture.

data.title: Discovering Hajo
data.description: An ancient pilgrimage center for three religions: Hinduism, Buddhism, and Islam.

data.title: Discovering Umananda Island
data.description: The smallest inhabited river island in the world, known for its Shiva temple and golden langurs.

data.title: Discovering Digboi
data.description: The Oil City of Assam, featuring the oldest operating oil well and refinery in Asia.

data.title: Discovering Sualkuchi
data.description: The textile center of Assam, famous for producing high-quality Muga and Pat silk.

data.title: Discovering Dehing Patkai National Park
data.description: Known as the Amazon of the East, it is the only rainforest in Assam with rich flora and fauna.

data.title: Discovering Tezpur
data.description: The City of Eternal Romance, rich in ancient mythology, ruins, and scenic parks along the Brahmaputra.

data.title: Discovering Mayong
data.description: A mysterious village in Assam historically famous as the cradle of black magic and sorcery.

data.title: Discovering Itanagar
data.description: The capital of Arunachal Pradesh, home to the historical Ita Fort and vibrant tribal culture.

data.title: Discovering Nuranang Falls
data.description: A stunning and relatively undiscovered 100-meter high waterfall located in the Tawang district.

data.title: Discovering Aalo
data.description: A scenic town situated at the confluence of Sipu and Yomgo rivers, known for its orange orchards.

data.title: Discovering Anini
data.description: A remote, misty town offering pristine landscapes, deep valleys, and raw natural beauty.

data.title: Discovering Pakke Tiger Reserve
data.description: A haven for hornbills and tigers, celebrated for its successful community-driven conservation models.

data.title: Discovering Shillong Peak
data.description: The highest point in Meghalaya, offering a breathtaking panoramic view of the entire city and waterfalls.

data.title: Discovering Elephant Falls
data.description: A popular three-tiered waterfall located just outside Shillong, accessible via scenic staircases.

data.title: Discovering Mawphlang Sacred Grove
data.description: An ancient, deeply revered forest preserved by local tribes, rich in rare medicinal plants.

data.title: Discovering Nartiang Monoliths
data.description: A massive collection of ancient megalithic stones erected to honor the old Jaintia kings.

data.title: Discovering Nohkalikai Falls
data.description: The tallest plunge waterfall in India, dropping spectacularly into a deep green pool below.

data.title: Discovering Siju Cave
data.description: Known as the Bat Cave, it features impressive limestone stalactites, stalagmites, and deep river passages.

data.title: Discovering Kachari Ruins
data.description: Mushroom-domed pillars and ruins dating back to the 10th century Kachari civilization in Dimapur.

data.title: Discovering Mount Saramati
data.description: The highest peak in Nagaland, located near the Myanmar border, offering a challenging alpine trek.

data.title: Discovering Benreu
data.description: An unspoiled village of the Zeliang tribe, acting as a living museum of primitive Naga culture.

data.title: Discovering Imphal War Cemetery
data.description: A well-maintained memorial honoring British and Indian soldiers who fell during the Battle of Imphal.

data.title: Discovering Keibul Lamjao National Park
data.description: The only floating national park in the world, home to the endangered Sangai deer.

data.title: Discovering Ima Keithel
data.description: Asia's largest all-women market, a massive bustling hub of commerce and Manipuri tradition.

data.title: Discovering Tharon Cave
data.description: A historically significant, deep cave network in Tamenglong, offering an exciting spelunking experience.

data.title: Discovering Phawngpui (Blue Mountain)
data.description: The highest peak in Mizoram, known for orchids, rhododendrons, and sweeping views of the blue-tinted hills.

data.title: Discovering Dampa Tiger Reserve
data.description: The largest wildlife sanctuary in Mizoram, featuring dense canopies and diverse endangered species.

data.title: Discovering Aizawl
data.description: The picturesque capital of Mizoram, beautifully perched on ridges overlooking the Tlawng river valley.

data.title: Discovering Ujjayanta Palace
data.description: A magnificent royal palace in Agartala, now serving as the state museum of Tripura.

data.title: Discovering Sepahijala Wildlife Sanctuary
data.description: A biodiversity hotspot known for its clouded leopards, spectacled monkeys, and a large botanical garden.

data.title: Discovering Jampui Hills
data.description: The highest hill range in Tripura, famous for its eternal spring-like climate and lush orange orchards.

data.title: Discovering Tripura Sundari Temple
data.description: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas, a deeply revered ancient temple built by Maharaja Dhanya Manikya.

data.title: Discovering Zuluk
data.description: A rugged, high-altitude hamlet in East Sikkim, known for the dizzying loops of the Old Silk Route.

data.title: Discovering Rumtek Monastery
data.description: One of the largest and most significant Tibetan Buddhist monasteries in Sikkim, rich in murals and artifacts.

data.title: Discovering Khecheopalri Lake
data.description: A sacred, wish-fulfilling lake heavily venerated by Buddhists and Hindus, naturally hidden in a dense forest.

data.title: Discovering Yuksom
data.description: The first capital of Sikkim, an important historical town that serves as the basecamp for the Dzongri trek.

data.title: Discovering Barsey Rhododendron Sanctuary
data.description: A spectacular sanctuary in Sikkim that transforms into a vibrant canvas of blooming rhododendrons in spring.

data.title: Discovering Tuting
data.description: An offbeat paradise in Arunachal Pradesh offering extreme white water rafting on the Siang River.

data.title: Discovering Daporijo
data.description: A quaint town surrounded by green hills, caves, and suspension bridges made of bamboo and cane.

data.title: Discovering Longleng
data.description: A rugged and enchanting district in Nagaland, home to the Phom Nagas and their unique traditions.

data.title: Discovering Jorhat Tea Estates
data.description: Known as the Tea Capital of the World, offering lush green estates and colonial-era bungalows.

data.title: Discovering Temi Tea Garden
data.description: The only tea estate in Sikkim, celebrated for its organic tea and breathtaking views of Mt. Kanchenjunga.

data.title: Discovering Tocklai Tea Research Institute
data.description: One of the oldest and largest tea research stations in the world, delving into the science of tea.

data.title: Discovering Monabarie Tea Estate
data.description: Recognized as the largest tea estate in Asia, featuring endless rolling plains of pristine tea bushes.

data.title: Discovering Halmari Tea Estate
data.description: A prestigious tea garden renowned globally for producing some of the highest quality orthodox Assam teas.

data.title: Discovering Corramore Tea Estate
data.description: Founded by Robert Logan, offering spectacular views of the Kaziranga ranges and premium tea blends.

data.title: Discovering Harmutty Tea Estate
data.description: A highly scenic tea estate framed by the backdrop of the Dikrong river and the Arunachal hills.

data.title: Discovering Margherita
data.description: Famous for its historic coal mines, plywood factories, and surrounding sprawling tea gardens.

data.title: Discovering Sonapur Tea Estate
data.description: Located just outside Guwahati, providing a quick, refreshing getaway into lush greenery and tea production.

data.title: Discovering Namsai Golden Pagoda
data.description: A magnificent Burmese-style Buddhist temple set amidst beautifully landscaped gardens.

data.title: Discovering Dong Village
data.description: The easternmost village in India, renowned as the place that receives the very first rays of the sun in the country.

data.title: Discovering Mayodia Pass
data.description: A breathtaking high-altitude pass near Roing, famous for heavy snowfall and pristine white landscapes.

data.title: Discovering Malinithan
data.description: An archaeological site containing ruins of a Hindu temple dating back to the 14th century.

data.title: Discovering Ward's Lake
data.description: An artificial lake surrounded by a lush botanical garden and cobblestone pathways in the heart of Shillong.

data.title: Discovering Don Bosco Museum
data.description: A major cultural center in Shillong providing a comprehensive overview of the indigenous cultures of Northeast India.

data.title: Discovering Kyllang Rock
data.description: A massive, dome-shaped granite rock offering panoramic views of the surrounding Khasi hills.

data.title: Discovering Jakrem Hot Springs
data.description: Famous for its hot sulphur water springs, believed to possess curative medicinal properties.

data.title: Discovering Dzuleke
data.description: A pristine, eco-friendly village offering a secluded retreat amidst dense forests and clear streams.

data.title: Discovering Mount Pauna
data.description: The third highest peak in Nagaland, providing excellent trekking trails and spectacular views of the Himalayas.

data.title: Discovering Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary
data.description: A remote sanctuary housing rare species like the tragopan pheasant, slender loris, and leopards.

data.title: Discovering Chuchuyimlang
data.description: An Ao Naga village famous for its grand celebrations of the Moatsu festival and scenic hilltop views.

data.title: Discovering Moreh
data.description: A bustling commercial town on the Indo-Myanmar border, acting as a melting pot of cultures and trade.

data.title: Discovering Singda Dam
data.description: The highest mud dam in the world, offering serene lake waters and surrounding green hills perfect for picnics.

data.title: Discovering Willong Khullen
data.description: Often called the Stonehenge of Manipur, featuring hundreds of mysterious, tall, erected stone monoliths.

data.title: Discovering Langthabal
data.description: A small hill adorned with relics of an old, historic palace and temples built by Maharaja Gambhir Singh.

data.title: Discovering Tam Dil Lake
data.description: A serene natural lake surrounded by tropical forests, popular for relaxation and a local prawn hatchery.

data.title: Discovering Palak Dil
data.description: The largest lake in Mizoram, rich in biodiversity, legendary myths, and an ideal spot for bird watchers.

data.title: Discovering Solomon's Temple
data.description: A magnificent, sprawling church structure in Aizawl made of white marble, uniquely designed by a local sect.

data.title: Discovering Murlen National Park
data.description: Often compared to the Amazon basin, this park has such dense canopies that sunlight rarely penetrates.

data.title: Discovering Chabimura
data.description: Famed for its massive, ancient rock carvings of Hindu deities on steep vertical walls along the Gomati River.

data.title: Discovering Pilak
data.description: An archaeological site holding 8th-12th century artifacts that signify the peaceful coexistence of Hinduism and Buddhism.

data.title: Discovering Trishna Wildlife Sanctuary
data.description: A protected area particularly known for its sizeable population of Indian Gaur (bison) and diverse primates.

data.title: Discovering Kamala Sagar
data.description: An expansive artificial lake abutting a prominent Kali temple near the Bangladesh border.

data.title: Discovering Goechala Trek
data.description: One of the most challenging and rewarding treks in India, leading right to the base of Mt. Kanchenjunga.

data.title: Discovering Thangu Valley
data.description: A picturesque, high-altitude alpine valley blanketed with snow in winters and wildflowers in spring.

data.title: Discovering Cholamu Lake
data.description: One of the highest lakes in the world, sitting just miles from the Tibet border with icy, crystal-clear waters.

data.title: Discovering Enchey Monastery
data.description: An important seat of the Nyingma order, nestled on a ridge above Gangtok providing calm and spiritual solace.

data.title: Discovering Kakochang Waterfalls
data.description: A magnificent waterfall cascading amidst tea gardens and rubber plantations near Kaziranga.

data.title: Discovering Panimoor Falls
data.description: A fierce, roaring waterfall formed by the Kopili river, creating an awe-inspiring natural spectacle.

data.title: Discovering Bhupen Hazarika Setu
data.description: Also known as the Dhola-Sadiya Bridge, it is the longest bridge in India over water, spanning the mighty Lohit river.
